data.title: nLight Inc reported Q2 earnings of 6 cents/share, beating estimates of a 9 cent/share loss. Quarterly sales of $61.735M exceeded estimates of $55.242M. Q3 sales guidance $62M-$67M vs $56.215M estimates. Keeney, nLIGHT President, expressed strong execution for the quarter.
data.description: Nlight Inc (NASDAQ: LASR) surpassed expectations with its second-quarter financial results as it reported earnings of 6 cents per share, outperforming the anticipated loss of 9 cents per share. The company's quarterly sales reached $61.735 million, exceeding the projected $55.242 million. Looking ahead, nLight anticipates third-quarter sales to fall within the range of $62.000 million to $67.000 million, surpassing the estimated $56.215 million. Scott Keeney, nLIGHT's President, expressed satisfaction with the strong performance in the second quarter of 2025, mentioning that revenue, gross margin, and Adjusted EBITDA exceeded predictions.

data.title: Block Inc. impresses analysts with upbeat Q2 earnings, total GPV grows to $66.6 billion. Stock rises post announcement.
data.description: Could Block Inc. (NYSE: XYZ) emerge as a surprising standout in this earnings season? While many anticipated tech giants to steal the show, Block's remarkable financial results have analysts abuzz. Despite an initial decline in stock value during early Friday trading, the company revealed strong second-quarter earnings on Thursday. XYZ is demonstrating resilience in the market. The news arrived amidst an eventful earnings period. Some significant points highlighted by analysts include RBC Capital Markets' take on Block. Analyst Daniel Perlin upheld an Outperform rating and raised the price target from $63 to $97. Block's total gross payment volume (GPV) surged by 7.5% year-on-year, accelerating from 4.4% in the prior quarter, reaching $66.6 billion, surpassing Street predictions of $66.1 billion according to Perlin's assessment. While Cash App GPV decreased to $2.4 billion, Square GPV increased to $64.3 billion. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Global Spectacle Lens Market expected to grow from USD 57.56 Bn in 2025 to USD 78.91 Bn by 2032, with a 4.6% CAGR. Single vision lenses lead, anti-reflective coatings dominate, myopia category holds 54.1% market share. North America leads with 43.6% in 2025. Vision disorders driving growth, while contact lenses and surgeries hinder. Increased awareness of eye health unlocking new growth opportunities.
data.description: The global Spectacle Lens Market is valued at USD 57.56 Bn in 2025 and expected to reach USD 78.91 Bn by 2032, showing a 4.6% CAGR from 2025 to 2032. Factors driving this growth include the rising prevalence of vision issues like myopia and hyperopia, technological advancements like blue light-blocking coatings, and increasing demand for customized eyewear. North America holds a market share of 43.6% in 2025, with Asia Pacific showing the fastest growth rate. Single vision lenses are the top-selling type, anti-reflective coatings dominate, and myopia category holds a significant market share. Global vision impairments are on the rise, with around 2.2 billion people affected worldwide, boosting demand for spectacle lenses. However, the market faces barriers due to the growing popularity of contact lenses and refractive surgeries among younger demographics. The increasing awareness of eye health among consumers presents new growth opportunities for the industry.

data.title: AvionTEq to participate in the 2025 ACPC in Atlanta from August 16-19,2025, under the theme "Driving Progress Through Innovation".
data.description: AvionTEq, a prominent provider of aviation testing equipment, tools, and services, will partake in the 2025 Air Carriers Purchasing Conference (ACPC) scheduled from August 16-19, 2025, at the Atlanta Marriott Marquis in Atlanta, GA. The event's theme, "Driving Progress Through Innovation," will see AvionTEq collaborating with global aviation experts to delve into the industry's upcoming trends. Participants can engage with AvionTEq during the conference.

data.title: FTC blocks Edwards Lifesciences Corp.'s acquisition of JenaValve due to monopoly concerns in $945 million deal for TAVR-AR market.
data.description: The Federal Trade Commission blocked Edwards Lifesciences Corp.'s proposed acquisition of JenaValve Technology, Inc., which would eliminate head-to-head competition in the U.S. market for TAVR-AR devices, used for treating aortic regurgitation. Edwards had already acquired JC Medical in July 2024 for $945 million. The FTC argues that this acquisition would limit patient access to lifesaving medical devices and stifle innovation, as it would combine the only two companies with ongoing clinical trials for TAVR-AR devices in the U.S. This move is significant as it could impact competition, innovation, and patient access in the TAVR-AR market.

data.title: PulteGroup earns 2025 Great Place To Work certification for the 7th time with 93% of employees saying it's great.
data.description: PulteGroup, Inc. has been recognized as a Great Place To Work® for the seventh time. The company, listed on NYSE as PHM and ranked as the third-largest homebuilding entity in America, received the certification based on feedback from employees. An impressive 93% of PulteGroup employees expressed that it is indeed a Great Place To Work, in stark contrast to the 57% average in typical U.S. companies. This recognition underscores the company's commitment to providing a positive and fulfilling work environment for its workforce.
